steps.title: Identify the isosceles triangle
steps.content: This problem uses isosceles-triangle properties rather than Pythagoras. Since $PQ = QR$, triangle $PQR$ is isosceles, so the base angles opposite the equal sides are equal: angle $P$ = angle $R$.

steps.title: Compute the requested value
steps.content:
With angle $P = 24°$, the base-angle equality gives angle $R = 24°$, and the diagram labels angle $R$ as $x°$:
$$x = 24$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
